meta.description: Guidelines for TypeScript Development targeting TypeScript 5.x and ES2022 output Triggers on: **/*.ts

original_definition.skill_description: Guidelines for TypeScript Development targeting TypeScript 5.x and ES2022 output Triggers on: **/*.ts

actions.name: Prefer readable, explicit solutions over clever shortcuts.
actions.description: Prefer readable, explicit solutions over clever shortcuts.

actions.description: Keep tests, types, and helpers near their implementation when it aids discovery.

actions.description: Name things for their behavior or domain meaning, not implementation.

actions.description: Run the repository's lint/format scripts (e.g., `npm run lint`) before submitting.

actions.description: Keep functions focused; extract helpers when logic branches grow.

actions.description: Express intent with TypeScript utility types (e.g., `Readonly`, `Partial`, `Record`).

actions.description: Debounce configuration-driven updates and dispose resources deterministically.

actions.description: Instantiate clients outside hot paths and inject them for testability.

actions.description: Encode untrusted content before rendering HTML; use framework escaping or trusted types.

actions.description: Patch dependencies promptly and monitor advisories.

actions.description: Handle secrets via the project's secure storage; guard `undefined` and error states.

rules.description: > These instructions assume projects are built with TypeScript 5.x (or newer) compiling to an ES2022 JavaScript baseline. Adjust guidance if your runt
Use pure ES modules; never emit `require`, `module.exports`, or CommonJS helpers.
Avoid `any` (implicit or explicit); prefer `unknown` plus narrowing.
Guard edge cases early to avoid deep nesting.
Never hardcode secrets; load them from secure sources.
Validate and sanitize external input with schema validators or type guards.
Avoid dynamic code execution and untrusted template rendering.
Reach configuration through shared helpers and validate with schemas or dedicated validators.
Avoid brittle timing assertions; prefer fake timers or injected clocks.

directives.description: Respect the existing architecture and coding standards.
Extend current abstractions before inventing new ones.
Prioritize maintainability and clarity, short methods and classes, clean code.
Target TypeScript 5.x / ES2022 and prefer native features over polyfills.
Follow the repository's folder and responsibility layout for new code.
Use kebab-case filenames (e.g., `user-session.ts`, `data-service.ts`) unless told otherwise.
Reuse or extend shared utilities before adding new ones.
Use PascalCase for classes, interfaces, enums, and type aliases; camelCase for everything else.
Skip interface prefixes like `I`; rely on descriptive names.
Match the project's indentation, quote style, and trailing comma rules.
